metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1376
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Authors overexpressed PDE4A5 in excitatory hippocampal synapses by means of an AAV viral injection. Expression was controlled by the CaMKII promoter. Over expression of PDE4A5 resulted in enhanced PDE activity and lower cAMP levels. When synaptic plasticity was assessed in these neurons was assessed by using chemical induction of LTP with forskolin and field recordings in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synapse, neurons overexpressing PDE4A5 showed a significant reduction in the fEPSP slope as compared to those neurons infected with a control construct carrying GFP, in agreement with a role of PDE4A5 in preventing cAMP-dependent synaptic plasticity.. Experimental description: From M&M:
Full-length rat PDE4A5 and mouse PDE4A1 constructs were based on GenBank accession numbers L27057.1 and M26715.

From Results:
Mice were bilaterally injected with AAV9-CaMKII-eGFP or AAV9-CaMKII-PDE4A5-VSV into the hippocampus.
